page: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance is bundled with 1890 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and supports Premium Storage. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong performance in multi-threaded decompression, reaching 183.01 GB/sec in bzip3, and single-threaded gzip compression at 30.92 GB/sec. It also exhibits strong capabilities in static web serving throughput at 56.21 GB/sec and image processing. However, the PassMark memory score is relatively weak, though cached read-only memory bandwidth reaches 2484.25 GB/sec.

The hardware configuration is optimized for workloads requiring a balance of compute power, memory, and high-speed local storage. It is highly capable for text processing, physics simulations, and small-to-medium language model inference, where it achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 355.44 tokens/sec on a 135M parameter model. The inclusion of a large local SSD provides qualitative cost efficiency for database operations, web serving, and intermediate data processing without requiring external storage attachments.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on a Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128.0 GB of RAM and is bundled with 1890 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix math. Benchmarks show strong performance in multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and small-model LLM inference, though memory performance in PassMark is relatively weak. This instance is well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and text processing workloads that benefit from high-speed local storage and balanced compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local SSD storage, optimized for balanced compute and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ds_v6 is a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon virtual machine with 128 GB RAM and 1890 GB local SSD storage, designed for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-speed Intel Xeon processors, integrated local SSD storage, and advanced matrix math acceleration.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
1890 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Strong performance in static web serving, decompression, and small-model LLM inference
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads
